item.name: iversion
item.description: Return Ion version information.

item.name: irandom
item.description: Generate a random number.

item.name: idetect
item.description: Detect a program.

input.name: Pointer to start looking in VAT
input.comments: Use the value in progPtr to start from the first program in the VAT.

input.name: Pointer to zero-terminated string for first line of data to match.

output.name: Pointer to data
output.comments: [83+] If the variable is held in Flash ROM, this pointer points to a temporary version held in RAM (Ion handles the unarchiving for you).

output.name: Success
output.comments: If no more variables were found, Z=1.

item.name: idecomp
item.description: Decompress data.

input.name: Pointer to place to decompress data to

output.name: Pointer to next byte of compressed data

item.name: directin
item.description: Direct input routine.

item.name: sendbytetios
item.description: Sends a byte out the link port in TIOS protcol.

item.name: getbytetios
item.description: Receives a byte from the link port in TIOS protocol.

item.name: version
item.description: Returns the MirageOS version

item.name: delayb
item.description: Delays using a halt loop.

item.name: quittoshell
item.description: Returns to MirageOS immediately, stack level does not matter (it is adjusted for you).

item.name: setupint
item.description: Sets up the Mirage interrupt.

input.name:
Interrupts to enable, with bits as follows:
0 Timer
1 GetKey
2 APD
3 Tasker
4 KeyDelay
5 Custom Interrupt

item.name: isprite
item.description: Draw a sprite (8 pixels wide, XOR) to the graph buffer.

item.name: ilsprite
item.description: Draw a large sprite (any height, multiple of 8 pixels width, XOR) to the graph buffer.

input.name: Width of sprite (bytes)
input.comments: One byte is 8 pixels, so for a 16 pixel wide sprite use a width of 2.

item.name: largespritehl
item.description: Draw a large sprite (any height, multiple of 8 pixels width, XOR) to the graph buffer.

input.name: Width of sprite (bytes)
input.comments: One byte is 8 pixels, so for a 16 pixel wide sprite use a width of 2.

item.name: igetpix
item.description: Get pixel information.

output.name: Pointer to address of pixel on graph buffer

item.name: ifastcopy
item.description: Copy the graph buffer to the LCD quickly.

item.name: fastcopys
item.description: Runs fastcopy, but preserves all registers.

item.name: putsprite8
item.description: Calls iputsprite, but sets the height to 8.

item.name: fastcopyb
item.description: Copies a 768-byte buffer to the display quickly.

item.name: fastrectangle
item.description: Draws a rectangle, quickly.

item.name: scrolld7
item.description: Scrolls graph buffer down by 7 lines.

item.name: multhe
item.description: Multiplies h*e quickly.

item.name: multhl
item.description: Multiplies h*l quickly.

item.name: multdehl
item.description: Multiplies de and hl.

item.name: cphlbc
item.description: Compares hl and bc.

item.name: cphlde
item.description: Compares hl and de.

item.name: rand127
item.description: Generate a random number between 0 and 127.

item.name: delprog
item.description: Deletes a program.

item.name: sendprog
item.description:
Sends a program over the link port to a waiting
calculator.

item.name: arcprog
item.description: Toggles archive status of a variable.

item.name: vatswap
item.description: Swaps two VAT entries.

item.name: renameprog
item.description: Renames a program.

item.name: getnext
item.description: Find the next program/list VAT entry.

item.name: getprev
item.description: Find the previous program/list VAT entry.

item.name: getchecksum
item.description:
Calculate the 16-bit checksum over a group
of bytes.

item.name: freearc
item.description:
Returns a string of the amount of free archive
memory.

output.name: Null-terminated string of amount of free archive memory

item.name: swapram
item.description:
Swaps two segments of RAM quickly through the
stack.

item.name: disprle
item.description:
Decompresses 768 bytes of RLE compressed data,
using $91 as the run flag.

input.name: Pointer to place to decompress data to

item.name: disprlel
item.description:
Decompresses RLE compressed data, using $91 as
the run flag.

input.name: Pointer to place to decompress data to

item.name: compstrs
item.description: Compare two null-terminated strings.

item.name: compstrsn
item.description: Compare two strings.

item.name: nextstr
item.description: Find the next string.

output.name: Pointer to byte after string's null terminator

item.name: gettext
item.description: Inputs text in small font.

input.name: Pointer to place to store string

item.name: gettextv
item.description: Inputs text in small font, with options.

input.name: Pointer to place to store string

Option byte, where the following bits set mean:
0 Do not display cursor
1 Do not display on screen/graph buffer
2 Do not allow numbers
3 Do not allow backspace/clear

item.name: disp3spaces
item.description: Displays 3 spaces in small font.

item.name: vputa
item.description:
Displays character in register a to both screen and
graph buffer.

item.name: vputsc
item.description:
Displays a string on the display and writes it to
the graph buffer using the TIOS _vputs routine.

item.name: vnewline
item.description: Increments (penRow) by 7, sets (penCol) to 0, and calls scrolld7 if necessary.

item.name: centertext
item.description:
Centres text (horizontally) on the screen and
displays it.

item.name: setvputs
item.description: Sets graph cursor location and displays text.

item.name: pixelonc
item.description: Clips and turns on pixel (a,e) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixeloffc
item.description:
Clips and turns off pixel (a,e) on graph
buffer.

item.name: pointxorc
item.description:
Clips and XORs (inverts) pixel (a,e) on
graph buffer.

item.name: pixelonhl
item.description: Turns on pixel (h,l) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixeloff
item.description: Turns off pixel (a,e) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixelxor
item.description: XORs (inverts) pixel (a,e) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixeltest
item.description: Tests state of pixel (a,e) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixeloffhl
item.description: Turns off pixel (h,l) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixelxorhl
item.description: XORs (inverts) pixel (h,l) on graph buffer.

item.name: pixeltesthl
item.description:
Tests state of pixel (h,l) on graph
buffer.

item.name: setpixel
item.description: Sets a pixel on the graph buffer.

item.name: fastline
item.description: Draws a line to the graph buffer quickly.

note.description:
The line style can be one of the following:
0 White
1 Black
2 XOR
3 Patterned
For a patterned line, the one-byte pattern is stored in (mlinebitmap)

item.name: fastlineb
item.description:
Draws a black line to the graph buffer
quickly.

item.name: fastlinew
item.description:
Draws a white line to the graph buffer
quickly.

item.name: fastlinex
item.description:
XORs (inverts) a line to the graph buffer
quickly.

item.name: fastlined
item.description:
Draws a dotted line to the graph buffer
quickly.
